title_en: Is One Of The Basic Principles Of
title_ar: من الأصول الأساسية في العقيدة الإسلامية
content_en:
FAITH (U$00L AL-EEMAA N) Belief in qadar (predestination), i s one of the basic principles of faith, without which a person's belief is incomplete. Muslim, narrated from 'Umar ibn al-Khattaab (�), describing how Jibreel (Gabriel) (>� ), questioned the Messenger (�) about eemaan (belief), in reply he (�) said: "It (eemaan) is to believe in Allah, His angels, His books, His messengers, and to believe in divine destiny (qadar), both the good and the evil thereof. " He (i.e., Jibreel) said: "You have spoken rightly. " 1 There are many texts which speak of the qadar of Allah or enjoin belief in qadar. These texts include the aayaat (verses): �Verily, We have created all things with Qadar [Divine Preordainments of all things before their creation as written in the Book of Decrees A l-Lau}J, al-Malifoo'f].� - (Qur 'an 54: 49) � $ 1_,)i; �.:;; �1 )'i SIG . . . � �- ..

And the Command of Allah is a decree determined.� (Qur 'an 33: 38) 1 Sharl} Al-Nawawi 'ala Muslim, 1 / 1 57. 4 . . . but [you met] that Allah might accomplish a matter already ordained [in His Knowledge] . . . � (Qur 'an 8: 42) 4 He has created everything, and has measured it ... exactly according to its due measurements.� (Qur 'an 25: 2) � CD �� �1i -sj� (!) lf_;:j Jr. ->j1 ¢» J'11 �� �1 � � 4Glorify the Name of your Lord, the Most High, Who has created [everything], and then proportioned it. And Who has measured [preordainments for everything even to be blessed or wretched]; and then guided [i.e. showed mankind the right as well as the wrong paths, and guided the animals to pasture].� (Qur 'an 8 7: 1-3) Muslim narrated in his Sa�ee� that Taawoos said: "I met some people from among the Companions of the Messenger of Allah who said that everything happens by qadar. He said, I heard 'Abdullah ibn 'Umar say: everything happens by qadar, even helplessness and resourcefulness, or resourcefulness and helplessness. " 2 Muslim also narrated that Abu Hurayrah (�) said: "The mushrikeen (polytheists) of Quraysh came and debated about qadar with the Messenger of Allah (�), then the following words were revealed: 2 Muslim, 4/2045, No. 2655. �The Day they will be dragged on their faces into the Fire [it will be said to them] : "Taste you the touch of Hell ! " Verily, We have created all things with Qadar [Divine Preordainments of all things before their creation as written in the Book of Decrees - A l-Lau� al­ Ma�foo�] ) (Qur 'an 54: 48-49) .

" 3 There are many similar texts. The texts which speak of the knowledge, power, will and creation of Allah also refer to His decree (qadar). Belief in qadar also includes belief in Allah's knowledge, will and creation, as we will see below, supported by the relevant texts. From the linguistic point of view - as stated by Raaghib al-A�fahaani, according to the report narrated from him by Ibn I:Iajar al-'Asqalaani - the word qadar implies ability or power, and the object of that ability, which exists in the knowledge of Allah.4 Allah, the Almighty, has absolute power (qudrah), and His power is not lacking in any way whatsoever. Among His Names - may He be blessed and exalted - are the names A lQaadir (the Able), A l-Qadeer (the All-Powerful) and A l-Muqtadir (Omnipotent, Able to do everything).

Power (Qudrah) is one of His Attributes. Al-Qaadir is the active participle of the verb qadara yaqdiru. A /­ Qadeer is a more intensive form, meaning the One Who does what He wants, in accordance with His wisdom, no more and no less. Therefore no one deserves to be described with this name except Allah, may He be glorified and exalted. Allah (�), says: 3 Muslim, 4/2046, No. 2656; Tinnidhi, 3/229. 4 Fat� al-Baari, l l/477. � ...

Yes, He surely is Able to do all things) (Qur 'an 46: 33) A l-Muqtadir is the active participle from the verbal form aqtadara, and is more intensive in meaning than Qadeer. For example, Allah (�), says: �In a seat of truth [i.e. Paradise], near the Omnipotent King [Ma/eek Muqtadir] [Allah, the One, the All­ Blessed, the Most High, the Owner of Majesty and Honour] .� (Qur 'an 54: 55) Imam Al)mad (may Allah have mercy on him) was asked about qadar. He said: "Qadar is the power of Allah. " 5 Ibn al-Qayyim said: "Imaam Al;tmad said that qadar is the power of Allah, and Ibn 'Aqeel liked this comment very much.

He said: 'This is indicative of Al)mad's precise and vast knowledge of the basic principles of religion (u�ool ad-deen). Abu'l-Wafaa' said, denying this means denying the power of the Lord to create and decree the actions of His slaves . "' Hence those who deny the qadar of Allah do not accept the power of Allah. Shaykh al-Islam Ibn Taymiyah (may Allah have mercy on him) said: "Whoever does not accept the opinion of the salafdoes not believe that Allah has power or that He is Able, like the Jahamiyah and those who follow them, and the Mu 'tazilah, both those who accept the idea of qadar but say that it means that man is compelled by qadar to do what he does, and those who reject the idea of qadar altogether. The essence of their opinion is that Allah is not Able, and that He does not have sovereignty, for sovereignty requires either 5 Majmoo ' al-Fataawa Shaykh al-Islam, 8/308. power, or the object of power, or both.

Whatever the case may be, He must have power, and whoever does not believe that He has true power does not believe that He has sovereignty. " 6 Those who deny qadar do not believe in Taw"l},eed, because those who deny qadar say that the creator of good is different from the creator of evil. Those among our ummah who believe this say that the sins which occur do not happen by the Will of Allah, and they may also say that He does not know about them. They say that all the deeds of living beings happen without His power or creation. So they deny that He has an irresistible Will and complete Power.

Hence Ibn 'Abbaas said: qadar is the essence of Taw�eed. Whoever worships Allah, the Almighty, Alone and believes in qadar has perfected his Taw�eed, but whoever worships Allah Alone but does not believe in qadar, destroys his Taw"l},eed by this disbelief. "7 Muslim scholars have unanimously affirmed the concept of qadar and stated that it is obligatory (waajib) to believe in it. There is no scholar among the scholars of Ahl as-Sunnah, the banners of guidance and lights in the darkness, who does not state that it is obligatory to believe in it, or state that the one who denies it is either guilty of bid 'ah or a fool. In his commentary on the a�aadeeth about qadar in [ja"l},ee"I}, Muslim, Al-Nawawi (may Allah have mercy on him) says: "In all of these a�aadeeth there is clear evidence that supports the view of Ahl as-Sunnah concerning the belief in qadar, and that all events, good and bad, beneficial and harmful, happen by the will and decree of Allah, the Exalted.

"8 6 Shifaa ' al- 'Aleel, 49. 7 Majmoo ' al-Fataawa Shaykh al-Islam, 8/258. 8 Shar� Al-Nawawi 'ala Muslim, 1 6/ 196. Elsewhere he said: "The definitive proof of the texts of the Quran and Sunnah, and the consensus (ijmaa) of the $al}aabah (the Companions) and the prominent figures among the earlier and later generations, overwhelmingly support one another to prove the qadar of Allah. "9 Ibn l:fajar (may Allah have mercy on him) said: "The opinion of all the salafis that all things happen by the decree of Allah, as Allah (�), says: "\'. � J_;.w ,,i� >.,. j. J.A; °'J� l r:O_j.j ,.,. G ..L;..c; •.:f >.>-,,.,,. !:'" »"> \:...J ,�\_j.- ,.,. :J l �:J. � 01,_, :i._ ,. • 7 ,,. �And there is not a thing, but with Us are the stores thereof.

And We send it not down except in a known measure [qadar]) (Qur 'an 1 5: 2 1) . " 10 9 Sharl} Al-Nawawi 'ala Muslim, 2/1 55 . 1 ° Fat}} al-Baari, 1 1 /478.
title_id: Qadar adalah Salah Satu Prinsip Dasar Keimanan
content_id:
Keimanan kepada qadar (takdir) adalah salah satu prinsip dasar keimanan, yang tanpanya iman seseorang tidak sempurna. Muslim meriwayatkan dari Umar bin Al-Khattab (ra), yang menggambarkan bagaimana Jibril (as) bertanya kepada Rasulullah ﷺ tentang iman, dan beliau menjawab: "(Iman adalah) kamu beriman kepada Allah, para malaikat-Nya, kitab-kitab-Nya, para rasul-Nya, dan Hari Akhir, serta beriman kepada qadar yang baik maupun yang buruk."

Hadis Jibril ini adalah salah satu hadis paling penting dalam Islam karena merangkum enam pilar iman secara komprehensif. Pilar keenam — beriman kepada qadar yang baik maupun yang buruk — menegaskan bahwa tidak ada yang terjadi di alam semesta ini kecuali dengan pengetahuan dan izin Allah.

Iman kepada qadar bukan berarti pasrah tanpa berusaha. Nabi ﷺ bersabda: "Beramallah, karena setiap orang dimudahkan kepada apa yang ia diciptakan untuknya." (HR. Bukhari dan Muslim). Ini menunjukkan bahwa iman kepada qadar justru mendorong seseorang untuk beramal dengan optimal, karena ia tahu bahwa sebab-sebab adalah bagian dari qadar itu sendiri.

Para ulama salaf sangat berhati-hati dalam membahas qadar karena ia adalah salah satu topik yang paling mudah menyebabkan kesesatan jika dibahas tanpa bimbingan wahyu. Mereka mengikuti metode: iman kepada apa yang Allah kabarkan, diam tentang apa yang tidak Allah kabarkan, dan tidak memaksakan akal untuk mencapai apa yang di luar kemampuannya.
content_ar: الإيمان (U$00L AL-EEMAA N) الاعتقاد in القدر (القضاء والقدر), i s one of the الأساسي principles of الإيمان, without which a person's الاعتقاد is incomplete. مسلم, narrated from 'عمر ibn al-Khattaab (�), describing how Jibreel (جبريل ﷺ) (>� ), questioned الرسول ﷺ (�) about eemaan (الاعتقاد), in reply he (�) said: "It (eemaan) is to believe in الله, His الملائكة, His الكتب, His الرسل, and to believe in divine destiny (القدر), both the الخير and the الشر thereof. " He (i.e., Jibreel) said: "You have spoken rightly. " 1 There are many texts which speak of the القدر of الله or enjoin الاعتقاد in القدر. These texts include the aayaat (الآيات): �Verily, We have created all things with القدر [Divine Preordainments of all things قبل their creation as written in the الكتاب of Decrees A l-Lau}J, al-Malifoo'f].� - (Qur 'an 54: 49) � $ 1_,)i; �.:;; �1 )'i SIG . . . � �- .. And the Command of الله is a decree determined.� (Qur 'an 33: 38) 1 Sharl} النووي 'ala مسلم, 1 / 1 57. 4 . . . but [you met] that الله might accomplish a matter already ordained [in His العلم] . . . � (Qur 'an 8: 42) 4 He has created everything, and has measured it ... exactly وفقاً لـ its due measurements.� (Qur 'an 25: 2) � CD �� �1i -sj� (!) lf_;:j Jr. ->j1 ¢» J'11 �� �1 � � 4Glorify the Name of your Lord, the Most High, Who has created [everything], and then proportioned it. And Who has measured [preordainments for everything even to be blessed or wretched]; and then guided [i.e. showed mankind the الحق as well as the الخطأ paths, and guided the animals to pasture].� (Qur 'an 8 7: 1-3) مسلم narrated in his Sa�ee� that Taawoos said: "I met some people from among the الصحابة of الرسول ﷺ of الله who said that everything happens by القدر. قال, I heard 'Abdullah ابن عمر say: everything happens by القدر, even helplessness and resourcefulness, or resourcefulness and helplessness. " 2 مسلم أيضاً narrated that أبو هريرة (�) said: "The mushrikeen (polytheists) of Quraysh came and debated about القدر with الرسول ﷺ of الله (�), then the following words were revealed: 2 مسلم, 4/2045, No. 2655. �The Day they will be dragged on their faces into the Fire [it will be said to them] : "Taste you the touch of النار ! " Verily, We have created all things with القدر [Divine Preordainments of all things قبل their creation as written in the الكتاب of Decrees - A l-Lau� al­ Ma�foo�] ) (Qur 'an 54: 48-49) . " 3 There are many similar texts. The texts which speak of the العلم, power, will and creation of الله أيضاً refer to His decree (القدر). الاعتقاد in القدر أيضاً includes الاعتقاد in الله's العلم, will and creation, as we will see below, supported by the relevant texts. From the linguistic point of view - as stated by Raaghib al-A�fahaani, وفقاً لـ the report narrated from him by Ibn I:Iajar al-'Asqalaani - the word القدر implies ability or power, and the object of that ability, which exists in the العلم of الله.4 الله, the Almighty, has absolute power (qudrah), and His power is not lacking in any الطريق whatsoever. Among His Names - may He be blessed and exalted - are the names A lQaadir (the Able), A l-Qadeer (the All-Powerful) and A l-Muqtadir (Omnipotent, Able to do everything). Power (Qudrah) is one of His Attributes. Al-Qaadir is the active participle of the verb qadara yaqdiru. A /­ Qadeer is a more intensive form, meaning the One Who does what He wants, in accordance with His الحكمة, no more and no less. لذلك no one deserves to be described with this name except الله, may He be glorified and exalted. الله (�), says: 3 مسلم, 4/2046, No. 2656; Tinnidhi, 3/229. 4 Fat� al-Baari, l l/477. � ... Yes, He surely is Able to do all things) (Qur 'an 46: 33) A l-Muqtadir is the active participle from the verbal form aqtadara, and is more intensive in meaning than Qadeer. على سبيل المثال, الله (�), says: �In a seat of الحق [i.e. الجنة], near the Omnipotent King [Ma/eek Muqtadir] [الله, the One, the All­ Blessed, the Most High, the Owner of Majesty and Honour] .� (Qur 'an 54: 55) الإمام Al)mad (may الله have الرحمة on him) was asked about القدر. قال: "القدر is the power of الله. " 5 ابن القيم said: "Imaam Al;tmad said that القدر is the power of الله, and Ibn 'Aqeel liked this comment very much. قال: 'This is indicative of Al)mad's precise and vast العلم of the الأساسي principles of religion (u�ool ad-deen). Abu'l-Wafaa' said, denying this means denying the power of the Lord to create and decree the actions of His slaves . "' ومن ثَم those who deny the القدر of الله do not accept the power of الله. Shaykh al-الإسلام Ibn Taymiyah (may الله have الرحمة on him) said: "Whoever does not accept the opinion of the salafdoes not believe that الله has power or that He is Able, like the Jahamiyah and those who follow them, and the Mu 'tazilah, both those who accept the idea of القدر but say that it means that man is compelled by القدر to do what he does, and those who reject the idea of القدر altogether. The essence of their opinion is that الله is not Able, and that He does not have sovereignty, for sovereignty requires either 5 Majmoo ' al-Fataawa Shaykh al-الإسلام, 8/308. power, or the object of power, or both. Whatever the case may be, He must have power, and whoever does not believe that He has true power does not believe that He has sovereignty. " 6 Those who deny القدر do not believe in Taw"l},eed, لأن those who deny القدر say that the creator of الخير is different from the creator of الشر. Those among our الأمة who believe this say that the الذنوب which occur do not happen by the Will of الله, and they may أيضاً say that He does not know about them. They say that all the deeds of living beings happen without His power or creation. لذا they deny that He has an irresistible Will and complete Power. ومن ثَم ابن عباس said: القدر is the essence of Taw�eed. Whoever worships الله, the Almighty, Alone and believes in القدر has perfected his Taw�eed, but whoever worships الله Alone but does not believe in القدر, destroys his Taw"l},eed by this disbelief. "7 مسلم العلماء have unanimously affirmed the concept of القدر and stated that يجب (waajib) to believe in it. There is no scholar among the العلماء of Ahl as-السنة, the banners of الهداية and lights in the darkness, who does not state that يجب to believe in it, or state that the one who denies it is either guilty of bid 'ah or a fool. In his commentary on the a�aadeeth about القدر in [ja"l},ee"I}, مسلم, النووي (may الله have الرحمة on him) says: "In all of these a�aadeeth there is clear evidence that supports the view of Ahl as-السنة فيما يتعلق بـ the الاعتقاد in القدر, and that all events, الخير and bad, beneficial and harmful, happen by the will and decree of الله, the Exalted. "8 6 Shifaa ' al- 'Aleel, 49. 7 Majmoo ' al-Fataawa Shaykh al-الإسلام, 8/258. 8 Shar� النووي 'ala مسلم, 1 6/ 196. Elsewhere قال: "The definitive proof of the texts of the القرآن الكريم and السنة, and the consensus (ijmaa) of the $al}aabah (the الصحابة) and the prominent figures among the earlier and later generations, overwhelmingly support بعضهم بعضاً to prove the القدر of الله. "9 Ibn l:fajar (may الله have الرحمة on him) said: "The opinion of all the salafis that all things happen by the decree of الله, as الله (�), says: "\'. � J_;.w ,,i� >.,. j. J.A; °'J� l r:O_j.j ,.,. G ..L;..c; •.:f >.>-,,.,,. !:'" »"> \:...J ,�\_j.- ,.,. :J l �:J. � 01,_, :i._ ,. • 7 ,,. �And there is not a thing, but with Us are the stores thereof. And We send it not down except in a known measure [القدر]) (Qur 'an 1 5: 2 1) . " 10 9 Sharl} النووي 'ala مسلم, 2/1 55 . 1 ° Fat}} al-Baari, 1 1 /478.
